text:
Diniyah education forms a backbone of the education system in the Muslim majority countries. It has a significant impact on the structure of the education system and also determines the social values of the societies. This study aims at exploring the history of diniyah education policy, its functions and how it is developed and implemented in the national education systems of the Muslim countries. The study employed qualitative research methods that involved interviews, observations and document analysis. The study findings revealed that the inclusion of religious education in the national curriculum has played a crucial role in promoting peace and dialogue in the multicultural and diverse societies. The study also revealed that the education policy has a far reaching impact beyond the religious, moral and spiritual development of the youth. It has a positive impact on the quality of education and also on the socio-economic development of the society. The study also revealed that the implementation of the education policy has its own challenges such as ensuring that the religious and secular education are integrated and that the youth are not exposed to extremist ideologies. The study therefore recommends that the diniyah education policy should be supported in order to promote peace, justice and equality in the society.

**Keywords:** Diniyah education; Education policy; Educational quality; Religious values; Morality; Social integration.
